message:
fix(fcp-oauth): redact attacker-controlled callback strings in AuthorizationError

REVIEW R2 P3 cross-review (cc_1) found that the e3474510 OAuth
Debug-redaction sweep (StateMismatch → [REDACTED]) left
`AuthorizationError` unredacted — a sibling-path leak for the same
class of attacker-supplied data.

Source: `AuthorizationError { error, description, error_uri }` is
constructed in oauth2.rs:807 directly from
`AuthorizationCallback::validate`, which pulls fields from the
user's browser-supplied callback URL query string. An attacker who
crafts the callback (e.g., via a phishing redirect) controls those
strings.

Two leak paths:

1. Display via `#[error("Authorization error: {error} - {description}")]`
   pushed the full attacker string into every `%` / `{err}` / `{:#}`
   consumer — tracing logs, anyhow chains, HTTP error bodies,
   downstream `impl fmt::Display`. Log-injection via CRLF, ANSI
   escapes, or HTML/JSON injection if a downstream renderer skips
   escaping.

2. Debug impl in e3474510 explicitly printed `error`, `description`,
   `error_uri` verbatim — contrast with the StateMismatch arm two
   cases above it which correctly uses `&"[REDACTED]"`.

Fix:

- Display format changes to `"Authorization error: {error}"` (short
  RFC 6749 §4.1.2.1 enum-like token only). The `error` field is
  bounded to a small set of well-defined values (`access_denied`,
  `invalid_request`, `unauthorized_client`, ...); attacker can
  influence WHICH code appears but not inject free-form text.
- Debug impl now matches StateMismatch's pattern: prints `error`
  verbatim, renders `description` and `error_uri` as `"[REDACTED]"`.
- Structured access for programmatic callers unchanged — they can
  still pattern-match the `description` / `error_uri` fields
  directly when they need the raw values, but they can no longer
  accidentally leak via `{err}` / `{err:?}`.

Two new regression tests:
- `authorization_error_display_redacts_callback_fields` — injects
  CRLF + attacker URL + fake admin marker and asserts Display
  strips all of them.
- `authorization_error_debug_redacts_callback_fields` — asserts
  Debug preserves `error` code but redacts `description` +
  `error_uri` (matching the StateMismatch shape).

Updated two existing tests (`authorization_error_empty_description`
and `authorization_error_all_fields_populated`) to pin the new
Display format and confirm structured access still works.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-oauth --lib — 620 pass with my change
(+1 net regression test), vs. 619 on baseline. Same 3 pre-existing
failures (state_mismatch_empty_strings, test_request_token_debug,
test_callback_debug) all assert the pre-redaction format on fields
that e3474510 already scrubbed; unrelated to this commit.

Cc_1 / SunnyMoose REVIEW-MODE R2 P3 cross-review
